Reform MRP Analysis in R

Author

John Arhin

Data

Using the site More in Common’s September MRP - More In Common, we download the September data from this url.

Data Munging

For each constituency, we find the party in second place and compute the following:

  • the margin_pp
  • the Swing_needed_for_runner_up_pp and
  • the Votes_to_flip_runner_up_per_50k_turnout.
Note

There is an aspect the project that involves constituency demographics.

We can see that Labour is the party that is predicted to be in second place the most number of times according to the MRP model.
